text.title: AI Incident Response Playbook Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure for authoring (and maintaining) an AI incident-response runbook.

step.name: enumerate-signals
step.input: Monitoring dashboards; alert definitions; cost dashboards.
step.action: List every signal the on-caller can observe: error_rate per route, p95 latency, hallucination flag count, refusal rate, $/hour spend, queue depth.
step.output: Signal table with units and dashboard URLs.

step.name: author-steps-with-thresholds
step.action: For each incident class, author steps with explicit signal + threshold + action + post-condition + time budget. No vague verbs.

step.name: put-escalation-at-top
step.action: Place escalation block (whom to wake at minute N) at the very top of the runbook.

step.name: schedule-drill
step.action: Schedule a drill within 90 days. Record drill_completed_at after execution.
step.decision-gate: If 90 days pass without a drill, surface in the next ops retro; the runbook is theatre.

step.name: publish-and-validate
step.action: Run scripts/validate-output.py; commit to ops repo; link from incident-tracker issue template.
